steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher claims some lakes show an increase in ice duration, contradicting the general declining trend from 1980 to 2006. The evidence must show at least one lake with more ice days in 2005–06 than in 1980–81.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — both Lake Baikal and Oulujärvi, which had more than 100 days of ice in the winter of 2005-06.: gives only a 2005–06 snapshot for two lakes with no 1980–81 comparison, so it cannot show an increase over time. ✗
  • Choice B — both Lake Baikal and Oulujärvi, which had fewer than 195 days of ice in the winter of 1980-81.: gives only a 1980–81 snapshot with no later comparison — cannot demonstrate a trend. ✗
  • Choice C — Lake Baikal, which had more days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it did in the winter of 1980-81.: directly shows an increase across both periods, providing exactly the evidence the researcher needs. ✓
  • Choice D — both Lake Kegonsa and Oulujärvi, which had more days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than they did in the winter of 1980-81.: factually wrong — Lake Kegonsa increased slightly, but Oulujärvi declined; because the choice says both lakes increased, it is false. ✗
C is correct.
